name: textparser
documentation:
# About

A text parser written in the Python language.

The project has one goal, speed! See the benchmark below more details.

Project homepage: <https://github.com/eerimoq/textparser>

Documentation: <http://textparser.readthedocs.org/en/latest>

# Credits

- Thanks [PyParsing] for a user friendly interface. Many of
  `textparser`'s class names are taken from this project.

# Installation

```python
pip install textparser
```

# Example usage

The [Hello World] example parses the string `Hello, World!` and
outputs its parse tree `['Hello', ',', 'World', '!']`.

The script:

```python
import textparser
from textparser import Sequence


class Parser(textparser.Parser):

    def token_specs(self):
        return [
            ('SKIP',          r'[ \r\n\t]+'),
            ('WORD',          r'\w+'),
            ('EMARK',    '!', r'!'),
            ('COMMA',    ',', r','),
            ('MISMATCH',      r'.')
        ]

    def grammar(self):
        return Sequence('WORD', ',', 'WORD', '!')


tree = Parser().parse('Hello, World!')

print('Tree:', tree)
```

Script execution:

```text
$ env PYTHONPATH=. python3 examples/hello_world.py
Tree: ['Hello', ',', 'World', '!']
```

# Benchmark

A [benchmark] comparing the speed of 10 JSON parsers, parsing a [276
kb file][276 kb file].

```text
$ env PYTHONPATH=. python3 examples/benchmarks/json/speed.py

Parsed 'examples/benchmarks/json/data.json' 1 time(s) in:

PACKAGE         SECONDS   RATIO  VERSION
textparser         0.10    100%  0.21.1
parsimonious       0.17    169%  unknown
lark (LALR)        0.27    267%  0.7.0
funcparserlib      0.34    340%  unknown
textx              0.54    546%  1.8.0
pyparsing          0.68    684%  2.4.0
pyleri             0.88    886%  1.2.2
parsy              0.92    925%  1.2.0
parsita            2.28   2286%  unknown
lark (Earley)      2.34   2348%  0.7.0
```

*NOTE 1: The parsers are not necessarily optimized for
speed. Optimizing them will likely affect the measurements.*

*NOTE 2: The structure of the resulting parse trees varies and
additional processing may be required to make them fit the user
application.*

*NOTE 3: Only JSON parsers are compared. Parsing other languages may
give vastly different results.*

# Contributing

1. Fork the repository.

2. Implement the new feature or bug fix.

3. Implement test case(s) to ensure that future changes do not break
   legacy.

4. Run the tests.

   ```text
   python3 -m unittest
   ```

5. Create a pull request.

[276 kb file]: https://github.com/eerimoq/textparser/blob/master/examples/benchmarks/json/data.json
[benchmark]: https://github.com/eerimoq/textparser/blob/master/examples/benchmarks/json/speed.py
[hello world]: https://github.com/eerimoq/textparser/blob/master/examples/hello_world.py
[pyparsing]: https://github.com/pyparsing/pyparsing

modules.name: textparser
documentation: A text parser.

functions.name: textparser.choice
documentation:
Returns an instance of the fastest choice class for given patterns
`patterns`. It is recommended to use this function instead of
instantiate {class}`~textparser.Choice` or
{class}`~textparser.ChoiceDict` directly.

functions.name: textparser.markup_line
documentation:
Insert `marker` at `offset` into `text`, and return the marked
line.

```python
>>> markup_line('0\n1234\n56', 3)
1>>!<<234
```

functions.name: textparser.tokenize_init
documentation:
Initialize a tokenizer. Should only be called by the
{func}`~textparser.Parser.tokenize` method in the parser.

functions.name: textparser.replace_blocks
documentation:
Replace all blocks starting with `start` and ending with `end` with
spaces (not including `start` and `end`).

variables.name: textparser.MISMATCH
documentation: Returned by {func}`~textparser.Pattern.match()` on mismatch.

classes.name: textparser._String
documentation: Matches a specific token kind.

classes.name: textparser.Error
documentation: General textparser exception.

classes.name: textparser.TokenizeError

methods.name: textparser.TokenizeError.text
documentation: The input text to the tokenizer.

methods.name: textparser.TokenizeError.offset
documentation: Offset into the text where the tokenizer failed.

This exception is raised when the text cannot be converted into
tokens.

classes.name: textparser.GrammarError

methods.name: textparser.GrammarError.offset
documentation: Offset into the text where the parser failed.

This exception is raised when the tokens cannot be converted into a
parse tree.

classes.name: textparser.ParseError

methods.name: textparser.ParseError.text
documentation: The input text to the parser.

methods.name: textparser.ParseError.offset
documentation: Offset into the text where the parser failed.

methods.name: textparser.ParseError.line
documentation: Line where the parser failed.

methods.name: textparser.ParseError.column
documentation: Column where the parser failed.
This exception is raised when the parser fails to parse the text.

classes.name: textparser.Pattern

methods.name: textparser.Pattern.match
documentation:
Returns {data}`~textparser.MISMATCH` on mismatch, and anything else
on match.
Base class of all patterns.

classes.name: textparser.Sequence
documentation: Matches a sequence of patterns. Becomes a list in the parse tree.

classes.name: textparser.Choice
documentation:
Matches any of given ordered patterns `patterns`. The first pattern
in the list has highest priority, and the last lowest.

classes.name: textparser.ChoiceDict
documentation:
Matches any of given patterns. The first token kind of all patterns
must be unique, otherwise and {class}`~textparser.Error` exception
is raised.

This class is faster than {class}`~textparser.Choice`, and should
be used if the grammar allows it.

classes.name: textparser.Repeated
documentation:
Matches `pattern` at least `minimum` times. Any match becomes a
list in the parse tree.

classes.name: textparser.RepeatedDict
documentation:
Same as {class}`~textparser.Repeated`, but becomes a dictionary
instead of a list in the parse tree.

`key` is a function taking the match as input and returning the
dictionary key. By default the first element in the match is used
as key.

classes.name: textparser.ZeroOrMore
documentation:
Matches `pattern` zero or more times.

See {class}`~textparser.Repeated` for more details.

classes.name: textparser.ZeroOrMoreDict
documentation:
Matches `pattern` zero or more times.

See {class}`~textparser.RepeatedDict` for more details.

classes.name: textparser.OneOrMore
documentation:
Matches `pattern` one or more times.

See {class}`~textparser.Repeated` for more details.

classes.name: textparser.OneOrMoreDict
documentation:
Matches `pattern` one or more times.

See {class}`~textparser.RepeatedDict` for more details.

classes.name: textparser.DelimitedList
documentation:
Matches a delimented list of `pattern` separated by
`delim`. `pattern` must be matched at least once. Any match
becomes a list in the parse tree, excluding the delimiters.

classes.name: textparser.Optional
documentation:
Matches `pattern` zero or one times. Becomes a list in the parse
tree, empty on mismatch.

classes.name: textparser.Any
documentation: Matches any token.

classes.name: textparser.AnyUntil
documentation:
Matches any token until given pattern is found. Becomes a list in
the parse tree, not including the given pattern match.

classes.name: textparser.And
documentation:
Matches `pattern`, without consuming any tokens. Any match becomes
an empty list in the parse tree.

classes.name: textparser.Not
documentation:
Matches if `pattern` does not match. Any match becomes an empty
list in the parse tree.

Just like {class}`~textparser.And`, no tokens are consumed.

classes.name: textparser.NoMatch
documentation: Never matches anything.

classes.name: textparser.Tag
documentation:
Tags any matched `pattern` with name `name`. Becomes a two-tuple of
`name` and match in the parse tree.

classes.name: textparser.Forward
documentation:
Forward declaration of a pattern.

```python
>>> foo = Forward()
>>> foo <<= Sequence('NUMBER')
```

classes.name: textparser.Grammar
documentation: Creates a tree of given tokens using the grammar `grammar`.

classes.name: textparser.Parser

methods.name: textparser.Parser.keywords
textparser.Parser.token_specs
documentation:
The token specifications with token name, regular expression, and
optionally a user friendly name.

Two token specification forms are available; `(kind, re)` or
`(kind, name, re)`. If the second form is used, the grammar
should use `name` instead of `kind`.

See {class}`~textparser.Parser` for an example usage.

methods.name: textparser.Parser.tokenize
documentation:
Tokenize given string `text`, and return a list of tokens. Raises
{class}`~textparser.TokenizeError` on failure.

This method should only be called by
{func}`~textparser.Parser.parse()`, but may very well be
overridden if the default implementation does not match the
parser needs.

methods.name: textparser.Parser.grammar
documentation:
The text grammar is used to create a parse tree out of a list of
tokens.

See {class}`~textparser.Parser` for an example usage.

methods.name: textparser.Parser.parse
documentation:
Parse given string `text` and return the parse tree. Raises
{class}`~textparser.ParseError` on failure.

Returns a parse tree of tokens if `token_tree` is `True`.

```python
>>> MyParser().parse('Hello, World!')
['Hello', ',', 'World', '!']
>>> tree = MyParser().parse('Hello, World!', token_tree=True)
>>> from pprint import pprint
>>> pprint(tree)
[Token(kind='WORD', value='Hello', offset=0),
 Token(kind=',', value=',', offset=5),
 Token(kind='WORD', value='World', offset=7),
 Token(kind='!', value='!', offset=12)]
```

The abstract base class of all text parsers.

```python
>>> from textparser import Parser, Sequence
>>> class MyParser(Parser):
...    def token_specs(self):
...        return [
...            ('SKIP',          r'[ \r\n\t]+'),
...            ('WORD',          r'\w+'),
...            ('EMARK',    '!', r'!'),
...            ('COMMA',    ',', r','),
...            ('MISMATCH',      r'.')
...        ]
...    def grammar(self):
...        return Sequence('WORD', ',', 'WORD', '!')
```
